canonical_definition: Based on the provided data, WikiBio is described as a benchmark dataset used for evaluating knowledge editing. The single available definition specifies its focus on "biographical information." The instrument is positioned as a way to operationalize and measure knowledge editing capabilities. For instance, one paper lists WikiBio among a set of datasets used to "edit different kinds of knowledge" ("Unlocking Efficient, Scalable, and Continual Knowledge Editing with Basis-Level Representation Fine-Tuning"). In this context, it is mentioned alongside other benchmarks like WikiDatarecent, WikiDatacounterfact, ConvSent, and ZsRE.
papers: Can Knowledge Editing Really Correct Hallucinations?
In-Context Editing: Learning Knowledge from Self-Induced Distributions
Physics of Language Models: Part 3.2, Knowledge Manipulation
Unlocking Efficient, Scalable, and Continual Knowledge Editing with Basis-Level Representation Fine-Tuning

definitions.text: A benchmark dataset for knowledge editing focused on biographical information.
definitions.paper: Unlocking Efficient, Scalable, and Continual Knowledge Editing with Basis-Level Representation Fine-Tuning

relationships.paper: Unlocking Efficient, Scalable, and Continual Knowledge Editing with Basis-Level Representation Fine-Tuning
relationships.evidence: we edit different kinds of knowledge: WikiDatarecent, WikiDatacounterfact (Cohen et al., 2024), WikiBio (Hartvigsen et al., 2024), ConvSent (Mitchell et al., 2022), and ZsRE (Yao et al., 2023). (Section 3.1)

iclr25: Unlocking Efficient, Scalable, and Continual Knowledge Editing with Basis-Level Representation Fine-Tuning
